what_it_does: Defines a 24x24 SVG icon showing two opposing horizontal arrows, one pointing right near the top and one pointing left near the bottom.
observations: Root SVG uses viewBox="0 0 24 24" with no fill.
Stroke is currentColor with stroke-width 1.5, round line caps, and round line joins.
The icon is made from two path elements.
The upper path draws a right-pointing arrow from x=3 to x=17 at y=9.
The lower path draws a left-pointing arrow from x=21 to x=7 at y=15.
standouts: Opposing arrows communicate swap or exchange.
The asset inherits color from surrounding text or CSS via currentColor.
conventions: Uses inline SVG path geometry rather than external image data.
Follows a stroke-only icon style with rounded terminals and joins.
Does not include embedded title, description, id, class, or accessibility attributes.
notes: A compact stroke icon for representing swap, exchange, or reversing between two directions.
questions: Should accessible labeling be supplied by the consuming UI?
Is the 1.5 stroke width intentional for this icon set?
purpose_vs_actual: Filename-aligned: the path name suggests swap arrows, and the SVG draws opposing horizontal arrows.
intention_signals: It appears intended to serve as a reusable UI icon for swap or exchange actions while inheriting visual color from its context.
contribution: Provides a small themeable bidirectional swap glyph that a fused system would keep as an action icon for exchange, switching, or reversing flows.
summary_for_embedding: This is a 24x24 stroke-only SVG icon with two opposing horizontal arrows representing swap or exchange.
intention_for_embedding: It was likely made to provide a reusable currentColor UI icon for swap or bidirectional transfer actions.
